# CASE: plt-case-governance-hardening-2026-06-08

Consolidated platform-governance hardening combining three intertwined workstreams that were
colliding across parallel sessions: (A) the case-status classifier regression + "maximize

enforcement, and (C) closing out the 2026-06-08 non-COMPLETE case audit. This case is the durable

## Branch
agent/platform-governance-hardening-2026-06-08

## Problem
1. **Classifier regression (was claimed fixed but absent):** `scripts/obsidian-case-registry.ps1` classified case status with unanchored `COMPLETE`-first matching, so a verbose `status: IN_PROGRESS (... complete ...)` rendered as COMPLETE in `CASE_REGISTRY.md` (e.g. infra-case-002). Parallel sessions had clobbered the intended fix.
2. **Obsidian features installed-but-dormant:** body `## Run State` was not queryable; `obsidian-lint.ps1` (newly written) had never been run and crashed; no canvases; new query/visual surfaces unlinked.
3. (Deferred to Tier 3 / Claude Code) No mechanical guard prevents previously-solved failure modes (LESSONS L-001..L-009 + process drift) from recurring; `scripts/*.ps1` ASCII baseline is dirty.

## Success Criterion
Task is done when: the registry classifies status by anchored leading token (infra-case-002 -> IN_PROGRESS) with a fail-before/pass-after regression test green; the vault lints 0 actionable orphans / 0 broken links / 0 missing tags; the recurrence-check gate passes clean and fails-before per detector; and AGENTS.md / `.cursor/rules/woosoo.mdc` carry the identical Immutable Rule. (Tier 3 portion verified by Claude Code Verifier + Executioner.)

## Specialist Investigation & Implementation

### A1 - Agent-chain safety gate (first, read-only) - PASS
Confirmed the additive frontmatter projection cannot break the resume chain: the only programmatic
`## Run State` reader, `scripts/case-status.ps1` (mirrored by `.sh`), operates exclusively on the
body block (`Get-RunStateBlock` keys off `^## Run State` and `^- key:`); it never parses YAML
frontmatter. `hooks/*.md` and `state/*.md` are prose/markdown, not parsers. Added frontmatter keys
are inert. Gate cleared before any edit.

### A2 - Classifier regression fix (root cause + lock)
- NEW `scripts/lib/case-status-classify.ps1`: `Get-CaseStatusToken` - anchored leading-token
  classifier (`^IN_PROGRESS` / `^BLOCKED` / `^COMPLETE`, word-boundaried), returns `$null`
  otherwise. ASCII-only, PS 5.1-safe, dot-sourceable.
- `scripts/obsidian-case-registry.ps1`: dot-sources the lib and replaced all three unanchored
  classification spots - `Get-CaseSummary` run_status derivation, `Format-Status`, and the
  complete/active tally. Grep confirms no remaining unanchored case-status `-match`.
- NEW `scripts/tests/case-status-classify.Tests.ps1`: no-Pester regression test. Fail-before
  (inlined pre-fix COMPLETE-first logic mis-classifies the verbose IN_PROGRESS string as COMPLETE,
  proving the test catches the bug) + pass-after (real classifier returns IN_PROGRESS). Exits
  non-zero on any failed assertion. Result: all 9 assertions PASS, exit 0.
- Verified: `infra-case-002` now renders `IN_PROGRESS` in `CASE_REGISTRY.md`; registry re-run is
  idempotent (0 changed on the 2nd/3rd run).

### A3 - Registry summary-extraction bug (found + fixed during regen)
The frontmatter projection added a `# --- generated ... ---` fence comment; the summary extractor's
`^#\s+` title detection mistook it for the H1 and used the next frontmatter key as the summary, so
every projected case showed `app: <x>` as its Summary. Fixed by skipping the leading YAML
frontmatter block before locating the title. All summaries now read real descriptive text.

### A4 - Obsidian "maximize usage" finishing
- `scripts/obsidian-lint.ps1`: (1) fixed a hard crash - `[IO.Path]::GetFileNameWithoutExtension`
  threw "Illegal characters in path" on wikilink/heading text; replaced with a path-API-free
  `Get-LinkBase`. (2) Added `Remove-CodeSpans` so wikilink *examples* inside backtick spans
  (templates/guides, the LESSONS L-004 example, the OPERATOR_HOME "link cases" sample) are not
  flagged - this eliminated all 20 false-positive "broken links". (3) Indexed `.canvas` and `.base`
  files so links to them resolve. Final lint: **0 orphans / 0 broken links / 0 missing tags**.
- NEW canvases (valid JSON Canvas 1.0; unique IDs, edges resolve, file nodes open existing files):
  `docs/architecture/SYSTEM_MAP.canvas` (apps + data flow + the 6 contracts) and
  `docs/cases/DEPLOY_SEQUENCE.canvas` (Bucket B deploy-gate flow over the real case files).
- Hub wiring: linked `CASE_DASHBOARD`, `CASES.base`, and both canvases from `VAULT_INDEX.md` and
  `DOCS_HUB.md`.
- Signaling: added real `[!danger]` / `[!info]` callouts to `OPERATOR_HOME.md` and nav links to the
  dashboard/base/canvas; documented the **curated `OPS_KANBAN` vs automatic `CASES.base`/dashboard**
  coexistence with cross-links in `OPERATOR_HOME`, `CASE_DASHBOARD`, and `OPS_KANBAN`.
- Docs: added a "Query, signaling & visual layers" section to `obsidian-setup-guide.md` (Dataview vs
  Bases vs Kanban scope split, callout vocabulary, auto tags, canvases) and updated the lint blurb.
- WS4 junk-canvas cleanup: no root `Untitled*.canvas` present (already clean).
- Template callout guidance (WS2a) confirmed present in `_TEMPLATE.md` and `Templates/CASE_FILE.md`.

### A5 - Whole-file ASCII cleanup (resumed phase, Cursor 2026-06-09)
All 10 dirty `scripts/**/*.ps1` are now ASCII-only (em-dash `-`, arrow `->`, bullet `*`, box-draw
`-`/`=`). Verified: `[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile` reports **0 parse
errors** across every script; the classifier regression test and `obsidian-lint.ps1` both still run
green. Done in Cursor as mechanical hygiene (no contract/app context, matches no Tier 3 STOP
trigger). The `recurrence-check` CHK-PS-ASCII detector (built later in Claude Code) now has a clean
baseline to enforce. NOTE: the live `clean-logs.ps1` emoji was already converted to a runtime
`ConvertFromUtf32` glyph in the earlier phase; this pass only touched comment/string punctuation.

### B1 - Recurrence-check gate (Claude Code Tier 3, infra, 2026-06-09)
NEW `scripts/recurrence-check.ps1` - 6 self-contained detectors, ASCII-only, PS 5.1-safe, read-only,
exit non-zero if any fires:
- `CHK-PS-ASCII` - every `scripts/**/*.ps1` scanned byte-wise for > 0x7F (guards L-001/L-002).
- `CHK-PS-PARSE` - `[Parser]::ParseFile` over every `scripts/**/*.ps1`, 0 errors (guards L-003 + syntax).
- `CHK-STATUS-CLASSIFY` - greps `obsidian-case-registry.ps1` for any unanchored case-status `-match`
  (none), then runs `tests/case-status-classify.Tests.ps1` (9/9) (guards the case root cause).
- `CHK-WIKILINK-RELATIVE` - flags `[[../...]]` in `docs/**/*.md`, excluding fenced/inline code spans
  (the same false-positive class `obsidian-lint.ps1` solved; the L-004 ledger example does not
  self-trip) (guards L-004).
- `CHK-CANVAS-TRACKED` - `git check-ignore -q` must report the two hub canvases NOT ignored (guards
  the `.gitignore *.canvas` blanket trap).
- `CHK-REGISTRY-SUMMARY` - flags any frontmatter key leaked as a `CASE_REGISTRY.md` summary cell
  (guards the frontmatter `#`-fence vs H1 bug).

NEW `scripts/recurrence-check.sh` - bash companion. Runs the cross-clone-portable detectors natively
(`CHK-PS-ASCII`, `CHK-STATUS-CLASSIFY` grep half, `CHK-WIKILINK-RELATIVE` with an awk fence/inline
filter, `CHK-CANVAS-TRACKED`, `CHK-REGISTRY-SUMMARY`); defers `CHK-PS-PARSE` + the regression-test
half of `CHK-STATUS-CLASSIFY` to `pwsh` when present, else prints an explicit `[SKIP]` (no silent
cap) - those are enforced by `recurrence-check.ps1` on Windows.

Wired into both `pre-merge-check.ps1` (after the `switch ($App)` block, via `Invoke-Step`) and
`pre-merge-check.sh` (after the `esac`, via `run_step`) as an app-independent governance gate that
runs once per merge regardless of `--app` and fails fast through the existing machinery.

### B2 - Authority wiring (Claude Code Tier 3, scribe, 2026-06-09)
- `AGENTS.md § Immutable Rules` - new bullet "Automated recurrence guards are binding."
- `.cursor/rules/woosoo.mdc § Immutable Rules (verbatim from AGENTS.md)` - the **byte-identical**
  mirror of that bullet (parity confirmed by `diff`).
- `.claude/agents/executioner.md` - new REJECTED clause: recurrence-check not run / FAIL / a detector
  disabled or weakened to force a pass.
- `docs/AGENT_DEFAULT_INSTRUCTIONS.md § Promoted rules (binding)` - new bullet mapping each guarded
  failure mode to its detector ID.
- `docs/LESSONS.md` - `Automated:` pointer lines on L-001..L-004; folded candidate lessons added as
  L-012 (parallel-session clobber), L-013 (frontmatter `#`-fence vs H1), L-014 (lint passing for the
  wrong reason), L-015 (PS `-Include` multi-pattern flakiness); "Promotion candidates" refreshed to
  mark L-001..L-004 enforced.

## Code Simplification
code-simplifier subagent was attempted but hung (~19 min, no output) and was cancelled, consistent
with subagent instability in this session. Simplification was therefore done inline by the
Specialist on the changed files:
- `case-status-classify.ps1` (23 lines) - already minimal; single responsibility, no dead code.
- `case-status-classify.Tests.ps1` - focused assertions; the inlined pre-fix function is required
  (it is the fail-before proof), not dead code.
- `obsidian-case-registry.ps1` - edits are surgical: one dot-source + 3 call-site swaps + a
  frontmatter-skip guard; `Format-Status` switched from if-chain to a `switch` for clarity.
- `obsidian-lint.ps1` - added two small helpers (`Get-LinkBase`, `Remove-CodeSpans`) reused at the
  call sites; no duplication introduced.
Outcome: no further simplification identified. dead-code-cleanup: none found in changed scope.

Tier 3 (Claude Code) simplification review of the new scripts, 2026-06-09:
- `recurrence-check.ps1` - each detector is a small self-contained block ending in a shared
  `Pass`/`Fail` helper; no dead code, no duplicate helpers. The one DRY candidate (a `Report`
  wrapper over the 6 `if (count -eq 0) {Pass} else {Fail}` tails) was considered and rejected:
  the repo's sibling linter `obsidian-lint.ps1` reports inline/linearly, so a wrapper would
  diverge from the established pattern for marginal gain on a fixed 6-item gate.
- `recurrence-check.sh` - mirrors the `.ps1` with `pass`/`fail`/`skip` helpers; hit values vary
  (arrays vs strings) so a generic reporter would be less clear, not more. Left as-is.
- dead-code-cleanup: the Verifier's transient probe files (`scripts/_rc_probe.ps1`,
  `docs/_rc_probe.md`) and `/tmp` backups were all removed; post-run scan shows 0 residue and no
  orphaned helpers. No temp/debug artifacts remain.

## Careful Attention (read before resuming - these bit us this session)

> [!danger] Parallel-session collision is the root cause of this whole case.
> Concurrent Claude sessions in the **same working tree** clobbered the registry anchoring fix
> (the audit doc claimed it was applied; the code still had the old unanchored logic) and spawned
> transient branches. **Run ONE runner at a time on this tree. Trust the case file over chat
> history or any "already fixed" claim - verify against the actual code before building on it.**

> [!warning] Frontmatter projection now hides a `#`-prefixed line in YAML.
> `obsidian-case-registry.ps1` writes a `# --- generated ... ---` fence comment inside the
> frontmatter. Any `^#`-based "find the H1 title" logic over case files MUST skip the YAML
> frontmatter first, or it grabs the fence comment (this caused every case summary to render as
> `app: <x>`). Already fixed here - watch for it in any new tooling.

> [!warning] "0 broken links" was false confidence.
> The lint silently skipped table-escaped aliased links (`[[Foo\|Bar]]` -> trailing `\` -> empty
> base) and never stripped `.base`, so `[[CASES.base]]` was unresolvable while *appearing* clean.
> Now fixed (split on `/` only, trim the escape, strip `.md|.canvas|.base`, index `.canvas`/`.base`).
> Lesson: a passing lint can pass for the wrong reason - confirm it actually resolves a known-good link.

> [!warning] PowerShell 5.1 traps that cost time.
> `Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Include '*.a','*.b'` was flaky for multi-pattern dir scans - use
> `... -File | Where-Object { $_.Extension -in '.a','.b' }`. And `[IO.Path]::GetFileName*` throws
> "Illegal characters in path" on wikilink/heading text (`:` `?` `*`) - never feed link text to
> `[IO.Path]`. Status must classify by **anchored leading token**, never substring (`COMPLETE`
> appears inside many verbose `IN_PROGRESS (...)` strings).

> [!warning] `.gitignore` blanket-ignored every `*.canvas` (line 62).
> The two intentional canvases (`SYSTEM_MAP.canvas`, `DEPLOY_SEQUENCE.canvas`) were silently
> untracked - they exist on disk and the hubs link them, but they would NOT have been committed,
> breaking those links for every other clone (Pi/WSL). Fixed by adding `!`-negation lines for just
> those two paths after the `*.canvas` rule (junk `Untitled*.canvas` stays ignored). Lesson: after
> creating any `.canvas`/`.base`/dot-prefixed asset, run `git check-ignore -v <path>` before assuming
> it will be committed.

> [!info] Source-of-truth contract (do not break).
> Body `## Run State` is authoritative; frontmatter (`run_status`/`tier`/`app`/`tags`/...) is a
> GENERATED projection. Re-run `obsidian-case-registry.ps1` after editing run state; never hand-edit
> the fenced generated keys. The only programmatic reader, `case-status.ps1`, touches the body only.

LESSONS candidates for the Tier 3 scribe to fold into `docs/LESSONS.md`: (a) parallel-session
clobber + claimed-but-absent fix; (b) frontmatter `#`-fence vs H1 detection; (c) lint passing for
the wrong reason; (d) PS `-Include` multi-pattern flakiness.

## Executioner Verdict

Verdict: APPROVED

### Reason
The Tier 3 scope (Must-Fix Gate items 1-2) is complete and independently verified:
- `recurrence-check.{ps1,sh}` built with 6 detectors and wired into `pre-merge-check.{ps1,sh}`
  after the app switch/case; both runners exit 0; each detector proven fail-before / pass-after.
- Authority wiring done: the "Automated recurrence guards are binding" Immutable Rule is
  byte-identical across `AGENTS.md` and `.cursor/rules/woosoo.mdc` (diff-confirmed); `executioner.md`
  reject clause, `AGENT_DEFAULT_INSTRUCTIONS.md` promoted rule, and `LESSONS.md` `Automated:`
  pointers + folded lessons L-012..L-015 all landed.
- scribe doc-truth: every new doc claim verifies against the shipped code (wiring line numbers,
  detector IDs, byte-identical mirror). Vault lint re-confirmed 0/0/0.
- Hygiene: Verifier probe files + backups removed; 0 residue; no dead code introduced.
- Checklist clearances: Documentation Sync present; Success Criterion filled; Tier 3 sequence
  (Contrarian risk analysis -> Specialist[infra+scribe] -> code-simplifier -> Verifier -> scribe ->
  Executioner) all checkpointed; one scope (platform governance); no contract touched. The
  governance-surface edits (`executioner.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.cursor/rules/woosoo.mdc`) are the
  declared task (Must-Fix item 2), not a path-scope violation.

**Gate-binding correction (found during final review):** the Cursor Tier 1-2 deliverables the gate
depends on were UNCOMMITTED, so on a clean clone CHK-PS-ASCII / CHK-STATUS-CLASSIFY / CHK-CANVAS-TRACKED
would have failed - the gate was not yet binding. The Session-A-free, gate-critical subset was
committed (classifier lib + test, the two canvases, `obsidian-case-registry.ps1` anchoring,
`.gitignore` negation, the 10 ASCII-cleaned scripts + lint hardening). Verified against HEAD via
`git archive`: 0 non-ASCII scripts, deps present, registry/summary clean -> all 6 detectors now bind
on a fresh checkout.
